most_recent_game.recap_text:
HAMMOND, La. -
A four-run sixth inning and an insurance tally in the eighth were nearly enough for Stephen F. Austin on Saturday afternoon from Pat Kenelly Diamond at Alumni Field. The Jacks carried a 6-5 lead into the eighth, added a tally in the frame, but allowed two in the home half before Southeastern Louisiana walked off the Jacks in the ninth to win game two 8-7 and take the series. The loss drops SFA to 11-9 overall with a 4-4 record in SLC play. SLU improves to 11-8 and 4-1 in conference action. Sunday's series finale is scheduled for 1 p.m.
Cody Templeton
went 5.0 innings allowing nine hits and five runs, before giving way to
Jack James
who pitched the sixth, seventh and eighth innings allowing the Lions' two runs in the frame.
Seanan Farney
worked the ninth as SLU recorded two hits to sandwich a sac bunt that moved a runner into scoring position.
Two runs scored for the Jacks in the fifth as a fielding error in
Jhett Creel's
at bat resulted in two runs as SFA cut into SLU advantage at 4-2. Ethan Peña homered on the first pitch he saw in the sixth and
Trey Acreman
took out 1-1 offering out to left field for a three-run shot that gave SFA a 6-5 lead. A sacrifice fly off the bat of
Cermodrick Bland
in the eighth provided a small cushion for the Jacks, but a two-run blast by the Lions in the home half of the inning tied the game at seven.
POSTGAME NOTES
SFA produced four hits for extra bases pushing the team's 2026 total to 57
Peña's bomb was his team-leading third of the season
Acreman's blast was his first of the season as he became the eighth different Jack to leave the yard this season
James and Templeton each fanned four with the latter running his team-leading total to 27
The Jacks struck out just four times after fanning a season-high 11 times on Friday
SFA stranded 11 SLU base runners and walked just three Lions
SECURE YOUR SEATS TODAY
Single game tickets
for all SFA baseball home games are available now. Fans interested in purchasing season ticket packages can secure their seats
here
. Don't miss out on all the exciting action from Pilgrim's Park this season!
THE JACKS HAVE GONE MOBILE
SFA Athletics' newest mobile phone application is designed to bring Lumberjack fans closer to the action than ever before. Available now on both the App Store and Google Play, the new SFA Athletics app delivers schedules, scores, rosters, news, ticket access, and exclusive content right to your phone. Fans can also receive push notifications, so they never miss a moment of the action. Download now:
Official SFA Athletics App
.
FOLLOW THE JACKS
For all the latest information on SFA baseball, visit
SFAjacks.com
. For up-to-the-minute updates, follow the Jacks on
X
,
Instagram
and
Facebook
.
